purpose: Identifies and corrects systematic overconfidence in the knowledge base on small-sample claims, providing calibration guidance for the entire framework
components: Systematic bias estimate: 1-2 points of overconfidence on claims derived from n≤6 episodes
Clopper-Pearson bounds for small-sample base rates
Fisher exact test for conditional probability claims (e.g., p≈0.10 on 1966-69 conditional base rates)
Confirmation bias detection: zero disinflationary scenarios after 47 iterations
current_assessment: The KB's central thesis is directionally supported but over-confidently calibrated. The systematic bias propagates through the entire framework. Highest-confidence findings are those supported by large cross-country evidence or contractual/arithmetic mechanisms.
limitations: Correction magnitude (1-2 points) is itself estimated
Does not identify which specific claims are most affected
Does not provide a replacement calibration methodology
May over-correct on claims with genuine structural underpinning
